commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=63ff4ab930f91bca25aced2d0cec250564e707e4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an iMessage monitor conversation-repair helper, tests, and monitor-provider integration to recover `chat_id=0`/empty watch payloads through `chats.list` plus `messages.history` before routing, otherwise dropping them.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection plus the linked issue show current main accepts `chat_id: 0` with empty chat identifiers and `is_group: false`, then routes it as `imessage:<sender>`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because this external PR has mock-only proof and changes privacy-sensitive iMessage routing; the contributor should revise the guard and provide real behavior proof.

target.title: fix(imessage): detect and recover anchorless watch payloads before routing
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix iMessage group link-preview watch payload losing its conversation anchor (chat_id=0, empty chat_guid/chat_identifier, is_group=false), which causes replies to be routed to the sender DM instead of the group.
- Add `isIMessageAnchorless()` detection guard and `repairIMessageConversationAnchor()` recovery via chats.list + messages.history RPC lookup.
- Fail-closed: if recovery cannot determine the real conversation, the event is dropped rather than routed to the wrong target.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: iMessage group link-preview watch payloads can lose their conversation anchor fields (chat_id=0, empty chat_guid/chat_identifier, is_group=false), causing the message to be routed to the sender DM instead of the group chat.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.22.0. The fix is pure TypeScript control-flow in the iMessage monitor provider and is platform-independent.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**: `npx vitest run extensions/imessage/src/monitor/conversation-repair.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**: 17 unit tests pass covering: anchorless detection (chat_id=0, empty guids, is_group=false), recovery via RPC lookup, fail-closed when recovery fails, and normal messages passing through unchanged.

**Observed result after fix**: Anchorless payloads are detected and repaired via RPC lookup before routing. If recovery fails, the event is dropped fail-closed rather than routed to the wrong DM. Normal (non-anchorless) messages are unaffected.

**What was not tested**: Live iMessage API integration with real BlueBubbles server. Multi-party group recovery with ambiguous chat matches.

Fixes #84470

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
